machinery_genes.quote: Examination of the membrane proteins of the wild-type and mxiD strains suggested that MxiD is an outer membrane protein. Amino acid sequence comparison revealed that MxiD is homologous to the YscC protein of Yersinia enterocolitica... These results indicate that MxiD is an essential component of the lpa secretion apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: Allaoui et al 1993 founding characterization; MxiD = SctC secretin (YscC/PulD homolog). Basal-body role also confirmed in Tamano 2002 ('a basal body made up of MxiD, MxiG, and MxiJ').

machinery_genes.quote: mxiM, part of the mxi-spa locus in the S. flexneri virulence plasmid, encodes an indispensable type III secretion apparatus component, required for both Ipa translocation and tissue culture cell invasion... The lipidated form of MxiM was detected primarily in the outer membrane... MxiM was found to be required for the assembly and/or function of outer, but not inner, membrane regions of Mxi-Spa.
machinery_genes.note: Schuch & Maurelli 1999. Outer-membrane lipoprotein that pilots assembly of the MxiD secretin; mapped to pilotin role in apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The type III secretion complex comprises a needle composed of MxiH and MxiI and a basal body made up of MxiD, MxiG, and MxiJ.
machinery_genes.note: Tamano et al 2002 names MxiJ as a basal-body component; MxiJ is the SctJ inner-ring lipoprotein (YscJ homolog) per the apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: Inactivation of mxiG abolished lpa secretion, which indicates that MxiG is an essential component of the Mxi-Spa translocon... the 42 kDa MxiG protein is associated with both the inner and outer membranes.
machinery_genes.note: Allaoui et al 1995 founding characterization. MxiG = SctD inner MS-ring; basal-body membership also stated in Tamano 2002 ('basal body made up of MxiD, MxiG, and MxiJ').

machinery_genes.quote: The type III secretion complex comprises a needle composed of MxiH and MxiI and a basal body made up of MxiD, MxiG, and MxiJ.
machinery_genes.note: Tamano et al 2002. MxiH = SctF major needle-filament subunit (YscF homolog).

machinery_genes.quote: The type III secretion complex comprises a needle composed of MxiH and MxiI and a basal body made up of MxiD, MxiG, and MxiJ.
machinery_genes.note: Tamano et al 2002. Brief maps MxiI to SctI inner rod (YscI/PrgJ homolog); Jouihri 2003 treats MxiH and MxiI together as 'the needle components'.

machinery_genes.quote: Inactivation of mxiK and mxiN, but not mxiL, resulted in the assembly of a non-functional TTSA that lacked the outer needle... we identified interactions between MxiK and Spa33 and Spa47 and between MxiN and Spa33 and Spa47.
machinery_genes.note: Jouihri et al 2003. MxiK = SctK C-ring base; interacts with the C-ring protein Spa33 and ATPase Spa47, required for needle assembly.

machinery_genes.quote: Inactivation of mxiK and mxiN, but not mxiL, resulted in the assembly of a non-functional TTSA that lacked the outer needle... we identified interactions between MxiK and Spa33 and Spa47 and between MxiN and Spa33 and Spa47.
machinery_genes.note: Jouihri et al 2003. MxiN = SctL ATPase stator (YscL/OrgB homolog); links the Spa47 ATPase to the C-ring (Spa33).

machinery_genes.quote: we revealed a Spa13 interaction with the inner-membrane MxiA and showed that overexpression of the large cytoplasmic domain of MxiA in the WT background shuts off secretion.
machinery_genes.note: Cherradi et al 2013. MxiA = SctV (InvA/YscV homolog), the major inner-membrane export-apparatus ring with a large cytoplasmic C-terminal domain.

machinery_genes.quote: the cytoplasmic proteins Spa47, Spa33, MxiK and MxiN... we identified interactions between MxiK and Spa33 and Spa47 and between MxiN and Spa33 and Spa47.
machinery_genes.note: Jouihri et al 2003 names Spa47 as the cytoplasmic ATPase of the TTSA; Spa47 = SctN (InvC/YscN homolog). Role as energizing ATPase also stated in Cherradi 2013 ('the ATPase Spa47').

machinery_genes.quote: Spa33, a cell surface-associated subunit of the Mxi-Spa type III secretory pathway of Shigella flexneri, regulates Ipa protein traffic... A spa33-null mutant was both noninvasive and unable to translocate the Ipas... These findings indicate that Spa33 is a mobile element within Mxi-Spa, which is required to control Ipa translocation.
machinery_genes.note: Schuch & Maurelli 2001. Spa33 = SctQ cytoplasmic C-ring / sorting-platform protein (SpaO/YscQ homolog); Cherradi 2013 refers to it as 'the C-ring protein Spa33'.

machinery_genes.quote: We identified a network of interactions between Spa13 and the ATPase Spa47, the C-ring protein Spa33, and the inner-membrane protein Spa40... our results support a dual role of Spa13 as a chaperone escort and as an export gate-activator switch.
machinery_genes.note: Cherradi et al 2013. Spa13 = SctO (YscO/Spa13) ATPase central stalk; named here as an apparatus component interacting with Spa47/Spa33/Spa40. Its chaperone-escort function is excluded; the structural stalk/switch role is the machinery role recorded.

machinery_genes.quote: One of the inner membrane components, Spa40, belongs to a family of proteins proposed to regulate the switching of substrate specificity of the export apparatus. We show that Spa40 is cleaved within the strictly conserved amino acid sequence NPTH.
machinery_genes.note: Deane et al 2008. Spa40 = SctU export-gate autoprotease (SpaS/YscU/FlhB homolog), cleaved at NPTH; the substrate-specificity switch of the apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: Spa32, encoded by one of the spa genes, is an essential protein translocated via the type III secretion system and is involved in the control of needle length as well as type III secretion activity... its function is interchangeable with that of Salmonella InvJ.
machinery_genes.note: Tamano et al 2002. Spa32 = SctP needle-length-ruler (InvJ/YscP homolog). Although it is itself secreted, it is a bona fide apparatus component (length control), not an effector; functionally interchangeable with Salmonella InvJ.

machinery_genes.quote: this study demonstrates that IpaD localizes to the TTSA needle tip, where it functions to control the secretion and proper insertion of translocators into host cell membranes.
machinery_genes.note: Espina et al 2006. IpaD = needle-tip protein. Listed as apparatus (tip complex), not effector, per the T3SS brief; it controls deployment of the IpaB/IpaC translocon.

machinery_genes.quote: The first proteins secreted are IpaD, IpaB, and IpaC, with IpaB and IpaC being inserted into the host cell membrane to form a pore for translocating late effectors into the target cell cytoplasm.
machinery_genes.note: Espina et al 2006. IpaB = translocon pore component (SipB/YopB family). Listed as apparatus per the T3SS brief (translocon proteins form the host-membrane pore), not as an effector, despite being secreted.

machinery_genes.quote: The first proteins secreted are IpaD, IpaB, and IpaC, with IpaB and IpaC being inserted into the host cell membrane to form a pore for translocating late effectors into the target cell cytoplasm.
machinery_genes.note: Espina et al 2006. IpaC = translocon pore component (SipC/YopD family). Apparatus (translocon), not effector, per the T3SS brief.

notes: System = the Shigella flexneri Mxi-Spa injectisome (T3SS), encoded by the ~20-gene mxi and spa operons on the virulence plasmid (pWR100/pINV; pWR501 in strain 2a 301). The apparatus was characterized in the model strains M90T (serotype 5) and YSH6000 / 2a; these are the same Mxi-Spa system as in 2a strain 301, so the gene names transfer directly (strain transfer noted per gene). Core injectisome documented gene-by-gene with verbatim naming quotes: secretin MxiD (SctC) + pilotin MxiM; basal-body rings MxiJ (SctJ) and MxiG (SctD); needle MxiH (SctF) + inner rod MxiI (SctI); export apparatus MxiA (SctV) and export-gate autoprotease Spa40 (SctU); cytoplasmic sorting platform / C-ring Spa33 (SctQ), C-ring base MxiK (SctK), ATPase stator MxiN (SctL), ATPase Spa47 (SctN), stalk Spa13 (SctO); needle-length ruler Spa32 (SctP); and the tip/translocon (IpaD tip, IpaB + IpaC translocon pore), which the T3SS brief explicitly counts as apparatus even though they are secreted. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery: secreted effectors (IpaA, IpgB1/B2, IpgD, OspB-G, IpaH family, VirA, etc.); the secretion/translocation chaperones IpgC (IpaB/IpaC chaperone), IpgE, Spa15 (IpgB1/B2/IpaA chaperone) and Spa13's chaperone-escort function; and transcriptional regulators MxiE, VirB, VirF, VirA. The export-gate trio Spa24 (SctR/SpaP), Spa9 (SctS/SpaQ) and Spa29 (SctT/SpaR) are genuine apparatus genes in this operon but were OMITTED because no source located gave a verbatim sentence naming them individually as apparatus components (anti-hallucination rule); they remain in the operon and would be picked up by locus resolution. MxiL (a small mxi gene whose mutant is secretion-competent, Jouihri 2003) also omitted for lack of a clear component-naming quote. All quotes are verbatim from the cited PubMed/PMC abstracts or full texts; every PMID/DOI confirmed via get_article_metadata.
